_description: Priority scoring configuration for Recovery tasks. Computes a 0-100 numeric score from weighted factors. The score maps to the existing priority field: 0-25=low, 26-50=medium, 51-75=high, 76-100=critical.
_rationale: Recovery's work is relationship-driven, so revenue impact and relationship value carry the heaviest weights. Time sensitivity ensures deadlines don't slip. Strategic alignment prevents chasing low-value-but-urgent distractions.

scoring_weights._description: Each factor contributes a weighted percentage to the final score. Weights must sum to 1.0.

revenue_impact.description: Financial value at stake — either revenue to gain (agreements) or revenue to protect (retention).

scoring_bands._note: Raw value → normalized 0-100 score before weight is applied

relationship_value.description: Strategic importance of the relationship beyond immediate revenue. A key partner with ecosystem access scores higher than a one-off deal of equal dollar value.

guidance.81_100: Critical ecosystem relationship — loss would cascade

time_sensitivity.description: How urgent is action? Computed from deadline proximity and trigger type.

trigger_overrides._description: Certain retention triggers automatically boost time sensitivity regardless of deadline.

strategic_alignment.description: How well does this task align with Recovery's current strategic priorities? Prevents urgency from overriding importance.

tag_boost_rules.description: Explicitly flagged by Sean as important

priority_mapping._description: Maps computed numeric score to the existing priority enum field used by Mission Control.

type_specific_adjustments._description: Base score modifiers applied per task type BEFORE weighted scoring. Ensures retention tasks get appropriate urgency.

partnership.notes: Slight boost because partnerships have compounding long-term value that's hard to quantify in immediate revenue

retention.notes: Retention tasks get a built-in boost. Losing an existing relationship costs more than missing a new one (5-7x acquisition cost principle).

pr.notes: PR scores from time_sensitivity (press deadlines) and strategic_alignment. No blanket modifier.

recalculation_triggers._description: Events that should trigger automatic priority re-scoring.
recalculation_triggers.rescore_reason: Deadline proximity changes daily. A task that was medium yesterday becomes high today as its deadline approaches.
